search_queries_used: site:doh.gov.ae well-child developmental delay screening standard Abu Dhabi
site:dha.gov.ae developmental concern child guideline Dubai
site:publications.aap.org developmental surveillance screening clinical report
candidate_sources_rejected: AAP Promoting Optimal Development clinical report
NICE developmental follow-up of children born preterm
rejection_reasons: The exact AAP article and PDF returned crawl-prevention or HTTP 403 during direct access, so search-result text was not treated as opened exact evidence.
The NICE guideline is limited to children born preterm and was not applied to the broad baseline.
population_applicability: Partial: exact source covers children aged 1 month to 6 years in well-child screening, not every school-age or diagnosed developmental disorder presentation.
setting_applicability: Partial: source is an Abu Dhabi well-child screening standard; the baseline is a broad developmental-concern workflow.
UAE_applicability: Verified for DoH-licensed well-child services in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates; broader UAE application is not asserted.
recency_verification: Official revised V10 standard published December 2025 and opened on 2026-07-11; effective date is stated as TBD.
superseded_check: Current revised official DoH copy identified on 2026-07-11; inaccessible AAP text was not used as evidence.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports age-specific developmental milestone assessment, parent history, observation, screening, and documentation in the stated population.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the 1-month-to-6-year population and requires parent history, age-specific physical examination, vital signs, developmental milestones, screening, vaccination discussion, and EMR documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports birth, feeding, medical, hearing, vision, growth, and developmental milestone review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies age-linked weight, length or height, head circumference, growth plotting, feeding and medical history, developmental milestones, screening, immunization review, and caregiver counselling.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports parent concern, walking, speech, social, developmental-screening, and referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines developmental and autism screening prompts, parent concern, walking, speech and social domains, and referral pathways for concerning screening results.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Directly supports documented caregiver education and screening or referral records.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires effective record keeping and documentation of screening findings, caregiver education, consent, and refusal.

unresolved_source_gaps: The source is limited to ages 1 month through 6 years and preventive screening rather than diagnosis.
Regression, seizures, safeguarding, school performance, fixed monitoring intervals, and the legacy developmental-delay diagnosis label remain unsupported by the reviewed exact sections.
The exact AAP report could not be directly opened and was not used.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
